task: I’ve been wanting to rebuild my social life and meet new people around Glendale, California, but I don’t want just a couple of random Meetup links. Please do a real browser-based sweep of recurring social options that someone could actually use over the next month or two, centered on Glendale and extending to nearby areas that are still practical like Burbank, Pasadena, Eagle Rock, Atwater Village, Silver Lake, or Los Feliz if needed. Find at least 12 viable ways to meet people, and make sure they come from at least 4 different public sources or categories such as Meetup groups, volunteer opportunities, community classes, hobby clubs, running groups, language exchanges, book clubs, board-game nights, or public event series. For each option, capture the name, what kind of people it seems aimed at, the neighborhood, whether it looks recurring or one-off, the next visible date or schedule if shown, the price or 'not shown,' and one short note on why it seems good for meeting new people rather than just attending passively. Open and compare the actual public pages, and also use maps/photos when helpful so you can sanity-check whether the locations feel practical and active. Then narrow it to the 6 strongest options for someone new in the area who wants genuine repeat social contact, variety, and a reasonable budget, and build a first-month plan using exactly 4 picks that together give a good mix of social styles and neighborhoods without requiring a ridiculous amount of driving. Keep the most useful evidence tabs open, including at least 6 final option pages and at least 2 map or venue pages, and finish with a concise recommendation on which single option looks best for easiest first-time socializing versus which one looks best for building longer-term community.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent identified 12 viable social options across multiple categories (board games, running, improv, library events, language exchange, and fiber arts) in the specified neighborhoods (Glendale, Burbank, Pasadena, Atwater Village). The agent verified these options through public pages (Meetup, Eventbrite, library calendars, and official websites) and confirmed their recurring nature and location. The list provided in the final response includes:
1. Geeky Games Friday Night Social (Burbank)
2. HomeState x Glendale Run Club (Atwater Village/Glendale)
3. Community Improv LA Brunch Jam (Glendale)
4. Pasadena Pacers (Pasadena)
5. Montrose Library Board & Card Game Club (Glendale)
6. Alhambra & Pasadena Spanish Conversation (Pasadena)
7. Knerdy Knitters & Crocheters (Burbank)
8. Brand Summer Music Series (Glendale)
9. NAMI Family-to-Family (Glendale)
10. TreePeople Volunteer Events (Los Angeles/Glendale area)
11. Polyglots and Language Lovers of LA (Online/LA)
12. Glendale Senior Recreation (Glendale)

All identified options are social in nature and located within the requested geographic scope.

R2: success - The agent identified 12 viable social options across at least 4 distinct categories as required by the prompt and rubric. The categories identified include:
1. Board-game nights (e.g., Geeky Games Friday Night Social, Montrose Library Board & Card Game Club)
2. Running groups (e.g., HomeState x Glendale Run Club, Pasadena Pacers)
3. Community classes/Improv (e.g., Community Improv LA Brunch Jam)
4. Language exchanges (e.g., Alhambra & Pasadena Spanish Conversation Group)
5. Fiber arts/Hobby clubs (e.g., Knerdy Knitters & Crocheters of the SFV)

The agent verified these through web searches and by opening the actual public pages (Meetup, Eventbrite, library event calendars, etc.), confirming they are recurring and active. The requirement for at least 4 categories is clearly met.

R3: success - The agent provided a table of 12 options in its final response. For each option, it included the name, audience/social fit, neighborhood, recurrence/schedule, price, and a note on why it is good for meeting people. The information is grounded in the web-fetched data (e.g., Geeky Games, Glendale Run Club, Community Improv LA, Pasadena Pacers, Montrose Library, and Spanish Conversation groups). The agent successfully verified these details through the browser-based sweep and provided the required fields for all 12 options.

R4: success - The agent successfully identified multiple social options across different categories (board games, running, improv, language exchange, library events). It verified these options by opening actual public pages (Meetup, Eventbrite, library event pages, and official organization websites). Furthermore, the agent performed sanity checks on the locations by opening Google Maps pages for key venues like "Geeky Teas & Games" and "The Glendale Room," confirming their existence and practicality. The agent maintained these evidence tabs throughout the process, satisfying the requirement to use public pages and map/venue checks to support its judgments.

R5: success - The agent successfully narrowed the research to 6 strongest options (Geeky Games Friday Night Social, HomeState x Glendale Run Club, Community Improv LA Brunch Jam, Pasadena Pacers, Montrose Library Board & Card Game Club, and Alhambra & Pasadena Spanish Conversation). The agent's final report explicitly justifies these selections based on the criteria of repeat contact (e.g., weekly/monthly recurring schedules), variety (board games, running, improv, language exchange), and budget (free or low-cost). The agent also built a 4-pick first-month plan and kept the required evidence tabs open.

R6: success - The agent successfully identified a wide range of social options and narrowed them down to a final selection. In the final response (Step 67), the agent explicitly builds a "first-month plan" using exactly 4 picks: 1) Geeky Games Friday Night Social (Burbank), 2) HomeState x Glendale Run Club (Atwater Village/Glendale), 3) Community Improv LA Brunch Jam (Glendale), and 4) Pasadena Pacers (Pasadena). The agent explains the mix of social styles (board games, running, improv, language/community) and neighborhoods (Burbank, Glendale, Atwater Village, Pasadena) and addresses the practicality of the locations. The agent also kept the required evidence tabs open (6 final option pages and 2 map pages).

R7: success - The agent successfully maintained 8 open tabs, which includes 6 final option pages (Geeky Games Meetup, HomeState Run Club Eventbrite, Community Improv LA Meetup, Pasadena Pacers events, Montrose Library game club, and Alhambra/Pasadena Spanish Meetup) and 2 map pages (Geeky Teas & Games and The Glendale Room). The agent's final report explicitly distinguishes the best option for easiest first-time socializing (Community Improv LA Brunch Jam, due to the low-pressure, structured nature of improv games) and the best option for longer-term community (Pasadena Pacers, due to their established infrastructure, coaching, and social events).
